title: Commodity markets are more than reflections of supply and demand fundamentals
section_path: Commodity markets are more than reflections of supply and demand fundamentals
section_path_text: Commodity markets are more than reflections of supply and demand fundamentals
summary:
Main
By Michalis Voutsinas
On this week last year, Doric's Weekly Insight examined the internal dynamics of OPEC+ through the lens of game theory, emphasizing Saudi Arabia's role as the cartel's dominant player within an increasingly fragmented producer alliance. At the time, the market's primary challenge was not supply insecurity but rather the opposite: managing an emerging surplus against a backdrop of weakening macroeconomic sentiment. Crude prices had been under sustained pressure, declining more than 20 percent since mid-January as sluggish Chinese demand, tighter financial conditions, and rising geopolitical fragmentation weighed on consumption expectations.
